name: Ask your business data in Slack and get AI-powered insights

parameters.content:
## Ask Your Business Data in Slack and Get AI-powered Insights via Databox MCP

Get instant answers about your business metrics directly in Slack. Mention the bot with any question - "How did Google Ads perform this week?" or "What's our YouTube watch time vs. last month?" - and the AI agent pulls live data from Databox via MCP and replies in the channel.

### How it works
`Slack Trigger (Bot Mention)` → `AI Agent (queries Databox MCP)` → `Reply in Slack`

### What you need
- Databox account with data sources connected → Free plan available: https://databox.com/?ref=n8n
- A custom Slack app (see setup instructions below)
- OpenAI or Claude API key

### Supported Data Sources
Google Ads • Google Analytics • Facebook Ads • YouTube • HubSpot • Salesforce • Stripe • And 100+ more → https://databox.com/integrations

## 1️⃣ Slack App + Trigger Setup

### What this section does
Listens for @mentions of your Slack bot in a specific channel and forwards the question to the AI Agent.





































### What you need to do ⚠️
### Step 1 - Create a Slack app 
1. Go to https://api.slack.com/apps and click **Create an App** → From scratch
2. Name it (e.g. "Databox Assistant") and select your workspace
3. Go to **OAuth & Permissions** → under Bot Token Scopes, add:
   - `app_mentions:read`
   - `chat:write`
4. Click **Install to Workspace** and authorize - you'll get a **Bot User OAuth Token** (starts with `xoxb-`)
5. Go to **Basic Information** → copy the **Signing Secret**

### Step 2 - Create credential in n8n 
1. In n8n, go to **Credentials** → Add credential → search **Slack API**
2. Paste the **Bot User OAuth Token** into Access Token
3. Paste the **Signing Secret** into Signature Secret
4. Save and select this credential in both the **Slack Trigger** and **Reply in Thread** nodes

### Step 3 - Register the webhook 
1. Copy the **Production URL** from the Slack Trigger node (expand Webhook URLs at the top)
2. Make the workflow **Active** (toggle top right in n8n)
3. In your Slack app, go to **Event Subscriptions** → toggle On
4. Paste the webhook URL - Slack will verify it automatically
5. Under **Subscribe to bot events**, add `app_mention`
6. Click **Save Changes**

### Step 4 - Add bot to channel 
1. In Slack, go to the channel you want to monitor
2. Type `/invite @YourBotName` to add the bot
3. Update the **Channel to Watch** field in this trigger node to your channel ID
   - Find your channel ID: right-click channel → View channel details → scroll to bottom

## 2️⃣ AI Agent + Databox MCP Setup

### What this section does
The AI Agent reads the user's Slack question, queries Databox via MCP for the relevant data, and formats a concise Slack-friendly reply.





































### What you need to do ⚠️
1. Click **OpenAI Chat Model** → add your `API key` credential
   - You can replace this with the **Anthropic Chat Model** node
2. Click **Databox MCP Tool** → set Authentication to `OAuth2` → authorize with your Databox account
3. Ensure the data sources relevant to your team's questions are connected in Databox


### How to connect Databox MCP Tool in n8n
@[youtube](892KtXhv-vI)

## 3️⃣ Slack Message

### What this section does
The AI agent delivers in Slack, augmented with intelligent analysis to provide deeper insights and actionable information.






































### What you need to do ⚠️
- Use the **same Slack API credential** as in the Slack Trigger node (the bot token, not a personal OAuth account)
- Ensure your bot has been invited to the channel with `/invite @YourBotName`
- **Optional**: Add an error message node if the agent returns no data

nodes.name: Slack Trigger
nodes.notes: Triggers when someone @mentions the bot - change channel ID to your channel

options.systemMessage:
You are a data assistant for Databox. A team member has asked you a question about their business metrics via Slack.

STEP-BY-STEP WORKFLOW:
1. Call list_accounts to get the account ID.
2. Call list_data_sources to discover all connected data sources.
3. Based on the user's question, identify the relevant data source(s) and metrics.
4. Fetch the relevant data using load_metric_data or ask_genie for the time period mentioned (default to last 7 days if not specified).
5. Calculate any comparisons or trends the user asked about.
6. Reply in a concise, Slack-friendly format.

FORMAT RULES:
- Keep the answer short and focused - this is a Slack message, not a full report
- Use *bold* for key numbers and metric names
- Use emojis to make it scannable (📊 📈 📉 💰 🎯)
- If comparing two periods, show the WoW or MoM % change
- Format numbers with commas. Use $ for currency, % for rates
- End with one short AI-generated insight or recommendation
- Do NOT include preamble, disclaimers, or sign-offs
- If the question is ambiguous, make a reasonable assumption and answer it

nodes.name: Post message
nodes.notes: Replies in the same Slack channel - use the same Slack API credential as the trigger
